messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That kind of discovery can sit quietly in the back of your mind even when you're trying not to think about it.

Have you had a chance to get it looked at by a doctor yet?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

Those details — firmness, no pain, and that it's still there after a few weeks — are exactly the kind of thing a doctor needs to hear in person. That's not something to sit with much longer.

Is there anything getting in the way of making that appointment?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That reluctance makes sense — a lot of people feel that way about doctors. And you might well be right that it's nothing serious.

But a lump that's firm, painless, and growing over a few weeks is one of those things that really does need a professional set of eyes on it, not because it's definitely something, but because only they can tell you for certain. What would make booking that appointment feel a little more manageable?
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

My honest read is that I'm not the right one to give you that read — not because I'm dodging, but because the same description fits things that are nothing and things that matter, and only an exam can sort that out.

What I can say honestly is that the combination you're describing — firm, painless, slowly growing, a few weeks in — is the kind of thing doctors are specifically trained to evaluate quickly and accurately. That's not me being evasive; it's me being straight with you.

The worry you're carrying right now is real, and a doctor's appointment is the only thing that actually resolves it.
